spec_match.rationale: The rewritten concept demonstrates revert-rule, not revert-layer: the current rule is ignored and values resolve from either an earlier rule in the same layer or a lower-priority layer.

interactive.rationale: Checkboxes and a select drive the live preview, generated CSS, rule stack, and computed source readout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index card and concept lede now both describe revert-rule behavior in a layered design system.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo covers unsupported-browser fallback plus the important cascade edge case where no earlier rule exists in the current layer.

references.rationale: A labelled references section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5146458504429568 and the CSS Cascade spec.

design_system.rationale: The rewritten page uses public/styles.css variables for colors and spacing, keeps controls accessible, and avoids static-card-only output.

summary: The concept has been rebuilt around accurate revert-rule semantics with feature detection, same-layer and previous-layer fallback modes, generated CSS, and the required ChromeStatus reference.
